What we do

One yard. Everything your boat needs.

Shrink wrap to springtime detail, weekend touch-up to full restoration — we keep Long Island boats protected, looking sharp, and ready to run.

Shrink Wrapping

Tight, vented, and built to last the season. UV-stable film and proper framing that sheds water and snow instead of pooling it — no flapping, no surprises in spring.

Detailing

Compound, polish, wax, and a deep interior clean that brings the gelcoat back to a mirror. Oxidation, salt, and waterline stains — gone.

Paint & Refinishing

Hull, topsides, and bottom done right. Color-matched finishes and barrier coats that stand up to sun, salt, and trailering instead of chalking out in a season.

Restoration

Tired boat, big plans. We bring neglected hulls and interiors back to life — gelcoat repair, hardware, brightwork, and the details that make her look new again.

Captain Services

Deliveries, sea trials, and a steady, experienced hand at the helm. We know these waters — let us move her or run alongside while you learn the ropes.

Winterize & Store

Lay her up the right way. Systems flushed and protected, fluids handled, and wrapped tight so you launch in spring without a project list waiting for you.

Since 2000

Long Island's boat people, for two decades.

We started wrapping and caring for boats on Long Island in 2000, and we've been at it every season since.

This isn't a chain or a side hustle. It's the same hands on your boat year after year — owners who'd rather do one job properly than ten jobs fast. Wrap a hull so it survives the winter. Bring a faded deck back to a shine. Get a neglected classic running and looking the part again.

You'll get a straight answer on what your boat needs, a fair price, and work that holds up. That's the whole pitch.

Good to know

Questions we hear on the dock.

When should I shrink wrap my boat?

Before the first hard freeze — usually mid-to-late fall on Long Island. Wrapping early beats the rush and keeps fall rain and falling leaves out of the cockpit. Reach out in late summer and we'll get you on the schedule.

What areas do you cover?

Long Island and the surrounding waters. Tell us where the boat sits — your slip, your driveway, or our yard — and we'll sort out the rest.

Do you only work on boats out of the water?

No. Detailing and many touch-ups can happen in the water or on the trailer; wrapping, bottom paint, and bigger refinish work are done on the hard. When you reach out, let us know the situation and we'll tell you what's possible.

Can you handle an older or neglected boat?

That's some of our favorite work. Gelcoat repair, oxidation, hardware, brightwork — if there's a good hull under there, we can usually bring it back. Send a few photos and we'll give you an honest read.

How do I get a price?

Fill out the quote form below or call/text 631-268-8955. The make, length, and what you're after are all we need to get you a number — no runaround.
